column1: Juan Soto leads the slate with elite hit reliability, batting leadoff on the road and holding a strong platoon advantage against Merrill Kelly. Soto’s high percentage of games with a hit underscores his consistency, and the park factor offers a slight boost. Xavier Edwards is also a steady option, thriving from the left side with a favorable matchup at home. Mickey Moniak and Ildemaro Vargas round out the top tier, both benefiting from advantageous splits.
